evidence_excerpt: After resection, the patient recovered well; headaches, photophobia, and associated symptoms completely resolved with no relapse or need for medications in the 7 months after discharge.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Her headache, photophobia, and other associated symptoms have been completely resolved, with no relapse or need for medications in the 7 months after discharge.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After the surgery, the patient showed a transient nerve palsy marked by numbness and drooping in the left lip.
The transient nerve palsy had resolved by her 2-month follow-up.
Headache, photophobia, and associated symptoms completely resolved with no relapse or need for medications in the 7 months after discharge.

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up explicitly documented to 7 months post-discharge (mid-term). No data beyond 12 months, so long-term outcomes are inferred from sustained mid-term resolution. Baseline comorbidities (e.g., COPD, hypertension) are not detailed in follow-up and were not used to downgrade functional status.
